basics.name: Vincent Palmer
basics.label: Staff Site Reliability Engineer & Platform Engineering Leader
basics.summary: Site Reliability Engineer & Platform Engineering leader with 30+ years experience scaling infrastructure from startups to Fortune 500 environments. Specialize in Kubernetes, distributed systems, and enterprise platform engineering with track record of successful architecture implementation.

work.name: Independent Consultant & Architect
work.summary: Advised early-stage AI/Fintech startup on technical architecture, cloud infrastructure, and security practices under strict NDA.
highlights: Designed foundational cloud architecture across 3 providers and a multi-region deployment model for a Series-A AI/Fintech startup, reducing projected infrastructure spend by ~40% before production launch.
Delivered security posture assessment covering zero-trust network design, supply chain security, and compliance readiness.
Prototyped AI-native development tooling now released as open source projects (Engram, Skylet).

work.name: MedTech Startup
work.summary: Architected and led end-to-end implementation of greenfield prescription delivery platform, serving 10,000+ daily users with 99.95% uptime.
highlights: Led cross-functional team of 8 engineers through complete platform lifecycle from concept to production deployment.
Directed architectural strategy for gematik-approved E-Rezept prescription management, achieving regulatory approval in 6 months.
Spearheaded French market expansion, implementing local compliance requirements and achieving 20% market penetration in first year.

work.name: Feedzai SA
work.summary: Served as company Kubernetes Subject Matter Expert (SME), architecting migration of core FinTech platform to K8s.
highlights: Pioneered Crossplane implementation for unified Infrastructure as Code platform, enabling seamless multi-cloud deployments across AWS, GCP, and on-premise with 40% reduction in provisioning time.
Transformed CI/CD and security paradigms, implementing software supply chain security (Attestations/SBOMs) and next-generation internal developer platform, reducing deployment time by 60%.
Established SRE practices including SLI/SLO design, error budget management, and automated incident response, reducing MTTR from 2 hours to 15 minutes.

work.name: Booming Games Ltd
work.summary: Built and led high-performing SRE, Test Automation, and Systems Administration teams (15+ engineers) ensuring 99.95% platform reliability for global B2B gaming platform serving 10M+ daily active users.
highlights: Engineered hybrid infrastructure spanning 5+ bare-metal datacenters and public clouds (AWS, GKE), implementing bare-metal Kubernetes clusters achieving 50% cost savings compared to managed cloud services.
Established comprehensive observability framework with Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK Stack, providing real-time insights into system performance and reducing incident detection time by 80%.
Implemented chaos engineering practices and automated failure recovery systems, improving system resilience and reducing unplanned downtime by 90%.

work.name: KIWI.KI GmbH
work.summary: Led complete zero-downtime migration of IoT infrastructure from legacy VMs to Kubernetes for apartment door access control system serving 10,000+ units.
highlights: Implemented comprehensive CI/CD pipeline from scratch using Jenkins and GitLab CI, reducing deployment time by 75% and eliminating manual deployment errors.
Executed critical Python 2 to Python 3 migration across entire codebase (50+ services), ensuring continued security support and improved performance by 30%.

work.name: ChartMogul Ltd
work.summary: Championed DevOps methodologies and automation across engineering department.
highlights: Championed DevOps methodologies and automation across engineering department, fostering an agile culture that measurably accelerated release cadence and reduced manual toil.
Managed a diverse environment including early-adoption Kubernetes, Docker, and traditional VMs.

work.name: Performance Horizon Group
work.summary: Established formal change and release management process.
highlights: Established a formal change and release management process from ground up, implementing automated deployments to improve reliability and velocity.
Architected and deployed a highly available IDS/IPS/NSM solution to enhance platform security.

work.name: Odobo Limited
work.summary: Architected and implemented a continuous delivery platform.
highlights: Architected and implemented a continuous delivery platform integrating JIRA and Bitbucket, automating the full software delivery lifecycle.
Led successful consolidation of multiple configuration management tools into a single, unified Salt-based platform, improving consistency and reducing complexity.

work.name: William Hill Online
work.summary: Managed all production and pre-production deployments in a high-stakes, PCI-DSS compliant online gaming environment.
highlights: Engineered novel deployment techniques that reduced application downtime and streamlined release process.

courses: Linux user since 1994, Advanced Systems Programming expertise throughout career
30+ years continuous professional development in emerging technologies

awards.title: NHS Approved Supplier
awards.summary: Achieved approved supplier status for UK National Health Service (NHS) and secured contracts for UK government and defence clients.

awards.title: Newcastle College Technical Instructor
awards.summary: Recognized as subject matter expert and served as technical instructor at Newcastle College, teaching advanced courses on ActionScript, Lingo, PHP.

awards.title: Community Engagement Recognition
awards.summary: Active member of Berlin tech community (c-base hackerspace) and wider cloud-native ecosystem. Attended multiple KubeCons (Europe) and Nix Conf.

projects.name: Engram
projects.description: Distributed persistent memory system for AI agents. Provides cross-session context, task tracking, reasoning chains, and knowledge graphs for autonomous agent workflows. Designed to solve the fundamental statelessness problem in LLM-based development pipelines.
highlights: Architected a graph-based memory model enabling agents to store and traverse context, reasoning, and decisions across sessions
Built CLI tooling and integrations for Claude and OpenCode, enabling AI-native development workflows at scale
Adopted internally to coordinate multi-agent pipelines across all personal and consulting projects

projects.name: Skylet
projects.description: Secure, extensible WASM-based plugin runtime for autonomous agents and microservices. Provides sandboxed plugin execution with capability-based security, FFI support, and a lifecycle management model suitable for multi-tenant agent deployments.
highlights: Designed a capability-based security model enforcing least-privilege execution for untrusted WASM plugins
Implemented FFI bridge enabling safe interoperability between host runtime and plugin workloads
Companion to the Engram memory system, enabling extensible agent tooling without compromising host security

projects.name: domain_exporter
projects.description: Production-grade Prometheus exporter for WHOIS domain metadata. Exposes domain expiry, registrar, and status information as Prometheus metrics, enabling alerting and dashboarding for domain fleet management.
highlights: 98+ GitHub stars — one of the most widely adopted domain monitoring tools in the Prometheus ecosystem
Used in production across hundreds of organisations for domain expiry alerting and SRE runbooks
Written in Go with full Prometheus exposition format compliance and configurable WHOIS provider support

projects.name: aethalloc
projects.description: Experimental Rust memory hypervisor implementing asynchronous metadata offloading, hardware-enforced spatial safety via guard pages, and virtual memory page compaction. Targets high-performance systems where allocator behaviour directly impacts latency and throughput.
highlights: Implemented lock-free allocation paths using Rust atomics and custom arena management for low-contention throughput
Hardware-enforced spatial safety using guard page placement to catch out-of-bounds access at the allocator level
Asynchronous metadata offloading decouples bookkeeping from the hot allocation path, reducing P99 latency

projects.name: nixernetes
projects.description: Enterprise Nix-driven Kubernetes manifest framework with declarative compliance enforcement and zero-trust security defaults. Generates auditable, reproducible K8s manifests from typed Nix expressions, eliminating YAML drift and enabling policy-as-code at the manifest layer.
highlights: Declarative compliance enforcement baked into manifest generation — policies are code, not post-hoc checks
Zero-trust security defaults applied automatically: network policies, pod security standards, and RBAC least-privilege
12+ GitHub stars; used as a reference implementation for Nix-native Kubernetes platform engineering

projects.name: vulnz
projects.description: Go vulnerability data aggregator built to address EU NIS2 and Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) compliance requirements. Aggregates 28 vulnerability and advisory providers including EU-specific feeds (EUVD, BSI CERT-Bund, CERT-FR, ENISA) alongside global sources (NVD, KEV, OVAL, CSAF), enabling organisations to meet mandatory vulnerability management obligations under EU regulation.
highlights: Aggregates EU-mandated sources — EUVD, BSI CERT-Bund, CERT-FR, ENISA CSAF — required under NIS2 and the EU Cyber Resilience Act
28-provider coverage including NVD, CISA KEV, EPSS scoring, SBOM integration, and supply chain security feeds
Designed to fill the compliance gap for EU-based organisations lacking a single tool covering both ENISA and global CVE intelligence

projects.name: sun50iw12p1 Linux Research
projects.description: Mainline Linux kernel porting and hardware enablement research for the Allwinner H713 (sun50iw12p1) SoC — the chip powering the HY300 Android projector. Reverse engineered device tree, U-Boot support, and driver integration to bring mainline kernel support to unsupported consumer hardware.
highlights: 27+ GitHub stars — primary public reference for H713 mainline Linux enablement
Reverse engineered clock, pinctrl, and display subsystems from downstream vendor kernel to produce upstream-compatible device tree
Research directly enabling community mainline kernel support for a widely-available but previously unsupported SoC

projects.name: nixos-gateway
projects.description: Modular, data-driven NixOS framework for building declarative routers, firewalls, and network infrastructure. Covers DHCP, DNS, WireGuard VPN, firewall policy, and routing — all expressed as composable Nix modules with reproducible, auditable outputs.
highlights: Fully declarative network stack: WireGuard, nftables firewall, DNS-over-TLS, DHCP — all as composable NixOS modules
Reproducible builds mean infrastructure state is always recoverable and auditable from source
Designed as a practical alternative to proprietary router firmware for homelab and small enterprise network management

projects.name: DOTS Family Mode
projects.description: Comprehensive parental control system for Linux with advanced eBPF-based monitoring. Provides time management, application blocking, content filtering, and encrypted local storage — built entirely on mainline Linux kernel primitives without requiring cloud connectivity or third-party agents.
highlights: eBPF collectors for deep kernel-level visibility into process, network, and filesystem activity without kernel module patching
Written in Rust with a Nix-driven build system; Gherkin BDD test suite for behaviour validation
Fully offline — all policy enforcement and storage is local, with no dependency on external services

projects.name: DOTS — Device Orchestration, Totally Simplified
projects.description: NixOS-based declarative device orchestration framework targeting desktops and embedded Linux systems. Provides a reproducible, composable foundation for managing Linux device fleets — from single-user desktops to multi-device home and small office environments.
highlights: Declarative device configuration with Nix flakes — full system state reproducible from a single source of truth
Modular architecture supporting desktop, server, and embedded targets from a shared NixOS module library
Parent project to dots-family-mode and dots-wallpaper, demonstrating a composable NixOS application platform

projects.name: Adaptive Multimedia Platform
projects.description: AI-powered multimedia processing platform with 14 intelligence features including Whisper (speech-to-text), YOLOv8 (object detection), Tesseract OCR, and OpenCV pipeline integration. Written in Rust with dual AGPLv3/Commercial licensing.
highlights: 14 AI intelligence features unified in a single Rust processing pipeline
Integrates Whisper, YOLOv8, Tesseract, and OpenCV as composable processing stages
Dual-licensed AGPLv3/Commercial — designed for both open community use and enterprise deployment

projects.name: nix-helm-generator
projects.description: Generate production-grade Helm charts from Nix expressions. Bridges the Nix ecosystem and Kubernetes tooling, enabling teams already using NixOS or Nix-based CI to generate auditable, reproducible Helm charts without maintaining YAML by hand.
highlights: Generates Helm charts from typed Nix expressions — eliminates YAML authoring and templating errors
Production-grade output with values schema generation, chart testing, and CI integration
Companion tooling to nixernetes for teams using Nix-native Kubernetes platform workflows

projects.name: D-Stack Notes
projects.description: In-depth analysis of Germany's Deutschland Stack (D-Stack) sovereign technology reference architecture. Gap analysis against CNCF tooling, BSI compliance requirements, NIS2 obligations, and open-source alternatives — documenting the practical path to digital sovereignty for German public sector and enterprise.
highlights: Comprehensive gap analysis between D-Stack reference architecture and current CNCF/open-source ecosystem
Maps BSI TR and NIS2 compliance requirements to concrete tooling decisions
Public reference for German public sector, enterprise architects, and EU digital sovereignty practitioners

projects.name: web-spec
projects.description: Production-ready Gherkin Feature Runner written in Rust with enterprise CI/CD integration, performance monitoring, and multi-channel notification support. Enables BDD acceptance testing as a first-class pipeline stage.
highlights: Full Gherkin feature file parsing and step execution in Rust — no JVM or interpreter dependency
Enterprise CI/CD integration with structured reporting, performance monitoring, and multi-channel alerting
Used as the BDD test layer in dots-family-mode and other personal projects

projects.name: garnix-insights
projects.description: CI/CD insights and observability tool for Garnix.io, the Nix-native continuous build platform. Provides CLI, server, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) interfaces for querying build history, cache hit rates, and failure analysis.
highlights: MCP server interface enables AI agents to query CI/CD state directly — a practical example of LLM-native tooling
CLI and server modes for human and programmatic access to Garnix build telemetry
Fills the observability gap in Nix-based CI pipelines for teams using Garnix as their build infrastructure

projects.name: systemd-status-leds
projects.description: Go service that subscribes to systemd D-Bus events for defined units and reports their status on a WS281x RGBW LED strip over SPI. Mainline kernel driver — no proprietary libraries required.
highlights: Real-time systemd unit health visualised on physical WS281x RGBW LED hardware via spidev
Pure D-Bus event subscription model — zero polling, minimal resource footprint
Demonstrates practical embedded Linux systems integration: D-Bus, SPI, mainline kernel driver stack
